hypothesis: A maintainability-aware harness can distinguish immediate task success from longitudinal codebase health by checking sequential changes for new regressions, public API drift, and docs drift.

metricDefinitions.name: Maintainability score
metricDefinitions.description: Average per-step score across immediate task success, verification checks, and absence of new regression signals.

metricDefinitions.name: Immediate task success
metricDefinitions.description: Whether each individual requested change appears locally complete.

metricDefinitions.name: New regressions
metricDefinitions.description: New lint, type, test, build, docs, or public API drift introduced across later steps.

metricDefinitions.name: Rollback required
metricDefinitions.description: Sequential changes that required rollback or repair to preserve repo health.

steps.description: Add a backwards-compatible SDK field and update the docs surface that describes it.
Update the example fixture that consumes the new field without changing package boundaries.
Remove dead helper code while preserving generated docs and public package metadata.
